reasoning: While programmatic signals show high text recall (91%), the LLM fidelity check reveals severe structural failures (score 0.5) and a logically broken reading order. The primary agenda table is corrupted, with content from Item 5 displaced below the table and appearing after Item 6, coupled with the omission of the critical GICC resource URL.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: The URL address 'https://it.nc.gov/gicc' following the label 'GICC Website:' is completely missing from the output. In the JSON, the paragraph at reading_order 12 only contains the prefix text 'GICC Website: ', and there is no subsequent text element for the actual URL (there is a gap in the reading_order between 12 and 14 where the URL should be). As this is the primary contact/resource URL for the council, its omission is a significant loss of information.

The table structure is significantly corrupted. In the source, Item 5 ('Committee Reports') includes a bulleted list in the 'AGENDA ITEM' column and a list of names in the 'GICC MEMBER or GUEST' column. In the JSON (element 7), Row 5 (Item 5) is missing its sub-list content and the third column is an empty string. Furthermore, Row 6 (Item 6) is included within the table structure in element 7, while the details for Item 5 are extracted as separate elements (8 and 9) following the table.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Item 5 in table: 'Committee Reports' followed by a bulleted list of committees and a corresponding list of member names in the third column.
concerns.output_quote: Element index 7, Row index 5: 'content': [{"text": "5. Committee Reports"}], 'cells'[2]: {"cell_type": "data", "content": [{"text": ""}]}

concerns.analysis: The reading order is logically broken due to the structural failure in Check S3. The table (element 7) includes Item 6 ('GICC Member Announcements'). The details for Item 5 ('Committee Reports' list and members) are placed in elements 8 and 9. Consequently, a reader navigating top-to-bottom will encounter the summary of Item 6 before the specific details of Item 5.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Item 5 details (Committee list and members) are logically followed by Item 6 (Member Announcements).
concerns.output_quote: Element 7 (Reading Order 7) contains Item 6. Element 8 and 9 (Reading Order 8 and 9) contain Item 5 details.
concerns.location: Main agenda table and subsequent list/paragraph, elements 7, 8, and 9

concerns.analysis: The visual organization of information in the HTML output differs significantly from the source PDF regarding item 5 of the agenda. In the source PDF, the committee reports and their respective presenters are organized within the table, maintaining a clear visual association. In the HTML output, the content for item 5 has been extracted and reorganized into a bulleted list and a string of names positioned below the table. This breaks the visual flow and organization of the agenda information.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source PDF shows '5. Committee Reports' within the table, with sub-bullets for committees in the 'AGENDA ITEM' column and names in the 'GICC MEMBER or GUEST' column.
concerns.output_quote: In the HTML screenshot, the table row for item 5 is mostly empty, and the sub-bullets and names are rendered as separate text blocks below the table frame.
concerns.location: Item 5 of the Agenda Table, Page 1

concerns.analysis: The agenda table is visually broken because the content of row 5 has been improperly handled. The source document lists presenters in the right-hand column corresponding to specific committees in the middle column. The HTML output renders the row with an empty right column and places the presenters' names in a single, unformatted line of text below the table. This makes the table data for this section unreadable as a structured set of information, as the association between the committee and the
concerns.source_pdf_quote: A table with three columns ('TIME', 'AGENDA ITEM', 'GICC MEMBER or GUEST'). Row 5 contains a list of 6 committees and 6 corresponding names in the right column.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML table has row 5 as '15 min. | 5. Committee Reports | [empty]'. The associated names 'Paul Badr Debbie Brannan Scott Lokken Dean Grantham John Farley Alex Rankin' appear as a flat string of text below the table.

concerns.analysis: The document contains a URL 'https://it.nc.gov/gicc' in the footer. In the output (element 12), the text 'GICC Website: ' is present, but the actual URL is missing entirely from the JSON data.

action_items: Reconstruct the agenda table to correctly align Item 5 committees with their presenters
Restore the missing GICC website URL following the 'GICC Website:' label
Correct the reading order so that all Item 5 details are processed before Item 6
